scope.branch: feature/T-020-m3-closeout-and-retrospective (created from main at the M3.2 closeout commit ff9010a; fast-forwarded into main; deleted per the branching strategy rule 7)
in_scope: Ship the M3 retrospective at retrospective-m3-project-registration.md (13 sections per the Milestone Closeout Standard § 4: delivered capabilities, deferred capabilities, technical debt, known issues, lessons learned, architecture changes, documentation changes, testing summary, validation results, implementation reports, commit range, readiness for M4-A, recommendations for the next milestone).
Produce the M4-A plan at .ai/plans/M4-A-infrastructure-process-execution.md (12 sections mirroring the M3 plan; Status: Awaiting Approval).

Update the project-continuity state per Rule 15: session.json (this file), tasks.json (T-020 → In Progress → Done; T-021 M4-A.1 promoted to Ready; T-007 M3 summary → Done), current.md (active milestone M3 → M3 closed; last completed task T-020; next recommended task T-021), task-board.md (M3 closeout added to Done Recently; T-021 in Ready; the M3 summary in Deferred archived), milestones.json (M3 Active → Done with closed_at 2026-07-11; M3 closeout slice delivered; M3 evidence block updated; M3 closes block records the m3 tag + the M3 closeout commit + the M4-A plan path + the M3 retrospective path).

Move M3 from Active to Done in .ai/state/milestones.json with closed_at: 2026-07-11 and the M3 evidence block updated with the M3 closeout's handoff, implementation report, retrospective, and the M3 closeout slice entry.
Create the m3 annotated milestone tag at the M3 closeout commit on main per the branching strategy rule 9. The tag's message references the M3 retrospective path.

Promote the first M4-A task (T-021 — the M4-A.1 infrastructure project skeleton) to Ready in .ai/state/tasks.json. The task is the first dependency-satisfied task the M4-A plan names; per the Milestone Closeout Standard § 8, the closeout does not invent a new task.

Validate the complete M3 milestone end-to-end: npm run css:build (exit 0), dotnet restore (exit 0), dotnet build (0 warnings, 0 errors), dotnet test (273 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped per ADR-016 / M4-D), dotnet format --verify-no-changes (exit 0), visual smoke on /projects (the Register a project button is enabled; the registration modal opens; registering a project navigates to the populated state; renaming updates the name; unregistering removes the project).

Update ROADMAP.md (§ 2 M3 row Active → Done; M3 paragraph updated; the M3 closeout row in the M3 slice table updated from 'M3.x — M3 retrospective' to 'Delivered (M3 closeout, 2026-07-11)') and § 3 (M3 DoD bullets: every DoD item checked satisfied; the Open action is M4-A's responsibility and remains explicitly out of scope; the M3 retrospective is named; the M4-A plan path is named).

Update .ai/plans/master-delivery-plan.md (§ 1 M3 row Active → Done (closed 2026-07-11); M3 last-stable-evidence column updated with the M3 closeout commit + the M3 retrospective path) and § 3 (M3 completion status Active → Done (closed 2026-07-11); M3 evidence list updated; M3 closeout slice row Summary entry → Delivered (2026-07-11)).
Write the M3 closeout implementation report at implementation-report-m3-closeout.md and the M3 closeout per-session handoff at .ai/handoffs/2026-07-11-m3-closeout.md (mirrored to latest.md).
Coherent commit on the feature branch: chore(m3.closeout): close M3 with retrospective, M4-A plan, and m3 milestone tag.
Fast-forward merge the M3 closeout feature branch into main per the branching strategy rule 6.
Delete the M3 closeout feature branch per the branching strategy rule 7.
Skip push (not authorised in this session). The push decision recorded in the implementation report is Staged for push.
out_of_scope: M4-A implementation. M4-A begins in a separate session after the M3 closeout. The M3 closeout produces the M4-A plan in Awaiting Approval and promotes the first M4-A task (T-021) to Ready; the M3 closeout does not begin the M4-A implementation. The Progressive Coding Rule applies: one task per session, 13-step lifecycle, stop after the coherent commit.
Provider creation. M3 closeout does not create providers; the first concrete providers land in M4-D.
Reordering accepted milestones. M0, M0.5, M1, M2 are Done and immutable in their order. M3 closeout does not modify the order. M3 is appended as the next Done milestone.
Modifying any source code. M3 closeout is a docs + workflow + state change; no source code is modified.
Modifying any test code. M3 closeout does not add or modify any test; the M3 closeout's test count is identical to the M3.2 closeout's (273 + 7 skipped).
Modifying any build configuration. M3 closeout does not modify any file under src/, tests/, package.json, tailwind.config.js, or Directory.Build.props.
Modifying any constitutional rule. M3 closeout does not modify AGENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, DECISIONS.md, STYLEGUIDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or .ai/workflows/milestone-closeout.md.
Modifying the M3 plans. The M3.1 plan (.ai/plans/M3-project-registration.md) and the M3.2 plan (.ai/plans/M3.2-project-registration-slice-2.md) are unchanged by the M3 closeout.
Pushing to the remote. Push is not authorised in this session; the remote (origin = https://github.com/maestroohk/ai-engineering-platform.git) is configured but pushing is not authorised.

Validation results: 273 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped (per ADR-016); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke green. No push (not authorised).

intended_next_action: Stop. The next session is the M4-A.1 implementation. The user approves the M4-A plan (.ai/plans/M4-A-infrastructure-process-execution.md) and begins the M4-A.1 implementation per the Progressive Coding Rule. Do NOT begin M4-A implementation in this session.
session_notes: M3 closeout implementation session (2026-07-11). The user's brief was the Next invocation after the M3.2 closeout — the end-to-end collapsed form of Continue + Approve + the 13-step Progressive Coding lifecycle. The first dependency-satisfied Ready task was T-020 (M3 closeout). The session executes the Milestone Closeout Standard (.ai/workflows/milestone-closeout.md) for M3, mirroring the M2.6 closeout pattern. The session produces: (a) the M3 retrospective at retrospective-m3-project-registration.md (13 sections, the second milestone retrospective in this repository, mirroring the M2 retrospective's structure with M3-specific evidence); (b) the M4-A plan at .ai/plans/M4-A-infrastructure-process-execution.md (12 sections, Status: Awaiting Approval, expanded from the existing summary entry in the task board and master delivery plan); (c) the M3 closeout plan at .ai/plans/M3-closeout.md (mirrors the M2.6 closeout plan's structure); (d) the project-continuity state update (session.json, tasks.json, current.md, task-board.md, milestones.json); (e) the ROADMAP.md and master-delivery-plan.md updates; (f) the M3 closeout implementation report at implementation-report-m3-closeout.md (15+ sections, mirrors the M2.6 closeout report); (g) the M3 closeout per-session handoff at .ai/handoffs/2026-07-11-m3-closeout.md (mirrored to latest.md); (h) the m3 annotated milestone tag at the M3 closeout commit on main. The session validates the M3 milestone end-to-end: 273 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped (per ADR-016); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke on /projects green. The session does NOT begin M4-A implementation (per the brief's 'Do not begin the following task' rule and the Milestone Closeout Standard § 10 anti-patterns). The session does NOT push (no remote authorisation). The session does NOT modify any source code, test code, build configuration, or constitutional rule. Zero deviations: the M3 closeout follows the Milestone Closeout Standard as-is (the standard is mature enough to be reused without modification; the M2.6 closeout's 'introduce the standard' is amortised).
